 31→    private val localeManagerWrapper: LocaleManagerWrapper,

 36→        private set

 43→        private set

 45→        private set

 47→        private set

 49→        private set

 51→    private val _onDatePicked = MutableLiveData<Event<Unit>>()
 52→    val onDatePicked: LiveData<Event<Unit>> = _onDatePicked
 53→    private val _onPublishedDateChanged = MutableLiveData<Event<Calendar>>()
 54→    val onPublishedDateChanged: LiveData<Event<Calendar>> = _onPublishedDateChanged
 55→    private val _onPostStatusChanged = MutableLiveData<PostStatus>()
 56→    val onPostStatusChanged: LiveData<PostStatus> = _onPostStatusChanged
 57→    private val _onUiModel = MutableLiveData<PublishUiModel>()
 58→    val onUiModel: LiveData<PublishUiModel> = _onUiModel
 59→
 60→    @Suppress("VariableNaming")
 61→    protected val _onToast = MutableLiveData<Event<String>>()
 62→    val onToast: LiveData<Event<String>> = _onToast

 64→    val onShowNotificationDialog: LiveData<Event<Period?>> = _onShowNotificationDialog
 65→    private val _onNotificationTime = MutableLiveData<Period?>()
 66→    val onNotificationTime: LiveData<Period?> = _onNotificationTime

 68→    val onNotificationAdded: LiveData<Event<Notification>> = _onNotificationAdded

 70→    val onAddToCalendar: LiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>> = _onAddToCalendar
 71→
 72→    open fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
 73→        editPostRepository = postRepository
 74→        val startCalendar = postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
 75→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 76→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar)

 78→    }

101→    }

103→    fun onDateSelected(year: Int, month: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) {
104→        this.year = year
105→        this.month = month
106→        this.day = dayOfMonth
107→        _onDatePicked.postValue(Event(Unit))
108→    }
109→
110→    fun onShowDialog(postModel: PostImmutableModel) {
111→        if (areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)) {
112→            val currentPeriod = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
113→            _onShowNotificationDialog.postValue(Event(currentPeriod))
114→        } else {
115→            _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.post_notification_error)))
116→        }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun updatePost(updatedDate: Calendar, postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
120→        postRepository?.updateAsync({ postModel ->
121→            val dateCreated = DateTimeUtils.iso8601FromDate(updatedDate.time)
122→            postModel.setDateCreated(dateCreated)
123→            val initialPostStatus = postRepository.status
124→            val isPublishDateInTheFuture = PostUtils.isPublishDateInTheFuture(dateCreated)
125→            var finalPostStatus = initialPostStatus
126→            if (initialPostStatus == DRAFT && isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
127→                // The previous logic was setting the status twice, once from draft to published and when the user
128→                // picked the time, it set it from published to scheduled. This is now done in one step.
129→                finalPostStatus = SCHEDULED
130→            } else if (initialPostStatus == PUBLISHED && postRepository.isLocalDraft) {
131→                // if user was changing dates for a local draft (not saved yet), only way to have it set to PUBLISH
132→                // is by running into the if case above. So, if they're updating the date again by calling
133→                // `updatePublishDate()`, get it back to DRAFT.
134→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
135→            } else if (initialPostStatus == SCHEDULED && !isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
136→                // if this is a SCHEDULED post and the user is trying to Back-date it now, let's update it to DRAFT.
137→                // The other option was to make it published immediately but, let the user actively do that rather than
138→                // having the app be smart about it - we don't want to accidentally publish a post.
139→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
140→                // show toast only once, when time is shown
141→                _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.editor_post_converted_back_to_draft)))
142→            }
143→            postModel.setStatus(finalPostStatus.toString())
144→            _onPostStatusChanged.postValue(finalPostStatus)
145→            val scheduledTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postRepository.id)
146→            updateNotifications(postRepository, scheduledTime)
147→            true
148→        }, onCompleted = { postModel, result ->
149→            if (result == UpdatePostResult.Updated) {
150→                updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
151→            }
152→        })
153→    }
154→
155→    fun updateUiModel(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
156→        if (postModel != null) {
157→            val notificationTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
158→            val publishDateLabel = postSettingsUtils.getPublishDateLabel(postModel)
159→            val now = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis - 10000
160→            val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
161→                ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
162→            val enableNotification = areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)
163→            val showNotification = dateCreated > now
164→            val notificationLabel = if (enableNotification && showNotification) {
165→                notificationTime.toLabel()
166→            } else {
167→                R.string.post_notification_off
168→            }
169→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(
170→                publishDateLabel,
171→                notificationLabel = notificationLabel,
172→                notificationEnabled = enableNotification,
173→                notificationVisible = showNotification
174→            )
175→        } else {
176→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.immediately))
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    fun onNotificationCreated(scheduleTime: Period?) {
181→        _onNotificationTime.value = scheduleTime
182→    }
183→
184→    fun scheduleNotification(postRepository: EditPostRepository, notificationTime: Period) {
185→        updateNotifications(postRepository, notificationTime)
186→        updateUiModel(postRepository.getPost())
187→    }
188→
189→    fun onAddToCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository) {
190→        val startTime = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated).time
191→        val site = siteStore.getSiteByLocalId(postRepository.localSiteId)
192→        val title = resourceProvider.getString(
193→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_title,
194→            postRepository.title
195→        )
196→        val appName = resourceProvider.getString(R.string.app_name)
197→        val description = resourceProvider.getString(
198→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_description,
199→            postRepository.title,
200→            site?.name ?: site?.url ?: "",
201→            appName,
202→            postRepository.link
203→        )
204→        _onAddToCalendar.value = Event(CalendarEvent(title, description, startTime))
205→    }
206→
207→    private fun getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository): Calendar {
208→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
209→        val dateCreated = postRepository.dateCreated
210→        // Set the currently selected time if available
211→        if (!TextUtils.isEmpty(dateCreated)) {
212→            calendar.time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(dateCreated)
213→            calendar.timeZone = localeManagerWrapper.getTimeZone()
214→        }
215→        return calendar
216→    }
217→
218→    private fun updateNotifications(
219→        postRepository: EditPostRepository,
220→        schedulingReminderPeriod: Period = OFF
221→    ) {
222→        postSchedulingNotificationStore.deleteSchedulingReminders(postRepository.id)
223→        if (schedulingReminderPeriod != OFF) {
224→            val notificationId = postSchedulingNotificationStore.schedule(postRepository.id, schedulingReminderPeriod)
225→            val scheduledCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().apply {
226→                timeInMillis = System.currentTimeMillis()
227→                time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated)
228→                val scheduledMinutes = when (schedulingReminderPeriod) {
229→                    ONE_HOUR -> -60
230→                    TEN_MINUTES -> -10
231→                    WHEN_PUBLISHED -> 0
232→                    OFF -> return
233→                }
234→                add(Calendar.MINUTE, scheduledMinutes)
235→            }

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The crash occurs at:

1. `PublishSettingsViewModel.start()` (line 74) calls `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it)` with the `postRepository`
2. `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar()` (line 209) accesses `postRepository.dateCreated`
3. `EditPostRepository.dateCreated` (line 86) does `post!!.dateCreated` — but `post` is `null`, causing the NPE

The `start` method on line 72-78:
```kotlin
open fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
    editPostRepository = postRepository
    val startCalendar = postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
        ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
    ...
}
```

Even though `postRepository` is not null (the `?.let` passes), the internal `post` field within `EditPostRepository` could be null. When `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar` accesses `postRepository.dateCreated`, it calls `post!!.dateCreated` which throws NPE.

The fix should be in `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar` to handle the case where `postRepository` doesn't have a post loaded (i.e., `hasPost()` is false), or we should guard the `dateCreated` access. Let me also check if there's a safe way to get the date:

The most appropriate fix is to make `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar` handle the case where the post hasn't been set in the repository. We should check `hasPost()` before accessing `dateCreated`, or we should handle the null post gracefully.

Looking at the `start` method more carefully: `postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }` — this only checks if postRepository is non-null, but doesn't check if the repository actually has a post loaded. The fix should guard against accessing `dateCreated` when `post` is null.

The cleanest fix is to update `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar` to check `postRepository.hasPost()` before accessing `dateCreated`:

236→            if (scheduledCalendar.after(localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar())) {
